credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Renew on provider token lifecycle, bind to the registered client, and revoke or rotate on authority-link invalidation.

source_refs.rationale: Raw authority access and refresh tokens live only in the governed secret boundary.
Authority tokens stay bound to the exact client and reporting-subject context.

usage_constraints: No raw token outside the governed vault boundary.
Queued sends must revalidate token version before use.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Export to the vault immediately after issuance and rotate on environment refresh or provider policy changes.

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap explicitly exports HMRC client identifiers and secrets into vault records.
Client identity is a first-class binding input and must remain stable per environment.

usage_constraints: Sandbox and production client secrets must never share one record.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Export to the vault on issuance, attach live rotation evidence, and never co-store with sandbox credentials.

source_refs.rationale: Vault export and rotation evidence apply to HMRC client identifiers and secrets.
Provider environment remains frozen on the authority binding, so live credentials must remain distinct.

usage_constraints: Live authority credentials require stricter approval and rotation evidence than sandbox credentials.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate on federation key rollover and provider admin-boundary changes.

source_refs.rationale: Federated identity is a governed security dependency that requires controlled key and admin material.

usage_constraints: Do not mix IdP tenant-admin secrets with application client secrets.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate on client lifecycle events, redirect URI changes, or policy-hardening cycles.

source_refs.rationale: Application clients are a provisioning output and therefore create managed secrets.

usage_constraints: Separate web, native, and service-principal client material where the IdP does so.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate API credentials on provider policy intervals; preserve domain-proof evidence separately from the key record.

source_refs.rationale: The delivery provider account and sender domain create provider credentials and domain-proof artifacts.

usage_constraints: Outbound mail identities must remain environment-scoped and redaction-safe.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate whenever callback endpoints or provider signing settings change.

source_refs.rationale: Webhook and callback configuration produces shared verification material.

usage_constraints: Webhook verification secrets must never be embedded in customer-visible templates or logs.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate on platform key expiry or device messaging project rollover.

source_refs.rationale: Device-messaging projects and keys generate platform credentials.

usage_constraints: Push payloads must remain redaction-safe regardless of provider choice.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate on workspace or environment rollover; keep alert-routing ownership separate from ingest credentials.

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap provisions error-monitoring project tokens.
Logs and telemetry sinks must redact secrets and full regulated payload bodies.
source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 6. Data protection, privacy, and cache safety

usage_constraints: Error-monitoring vendors must not receive raw authority tokens or unrestricted regulated payload bodies.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate on integration rollout or support-vendor security policy change.

source_refs.rationale: External helpdesk integration requires vendor API credentials when selected.

usage_constraints: External helpdesk sync must not widen visibility beyond customer-safe or operator-authorized context.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate with provider policy or on self-host worker identity replacement.

source_refs.rationale: Managed OCR projects create provider credentials; self-hosted runtimes require workload identity instead.

usage_constraints: OCR workers must not bypass malware-scan or retention controls when fetching source documents.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate on scanner provider rollover or self-host worker identity rotation.

source_refs.rationale: Managed scanning or self-hosted decision both require governed service identity.
Scan and publish-time visibility checks are mandatory before staged uploads can be delivered.

usage_constraints: Scanning credentials must stay partitioned from customer-facing download or preview credentials.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate on security posture change and record every admin-boundary change with audit evidence.

source_refs.rationale: Key and secret rotation must be auditable and fail closed on version-binding issues.

usage_constraints: Never bootstrap a vault with unmanaged shared credentials embedded in CI or repos.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate key-admin boundary on personnel or provider trust changes; key versions rotate per platform policy.

source_refs.rationale: Root key material and rotation are governed at a stronger security boundary.

usage_constraints: Root key custody must stay segregated from ordinary application runtime roles.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate app and migration roles separately and gate promotion on schema compatibility evidence.

source_refs.rationale: Provisioning the primary PostgreSQL store creates runtime and migration credentials.
Migration workflows require a distinct controlled credential posture.

usage_constraints: Operational readers must not share write-capable migration credentials.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate on store rollover or audit-path resegmentation.

source_refs.rationale: Provisioning the audit store creates dedicated credentials or service identities.

usage_constraints: Append-only evidence writers should not share mutable control-store credentials.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate on bucket-policy or delivery-binding changes.

source_refs.rationale: Object storage buckets create runtime access roles or keys.

usage_constraints: Separate quarantine writers from general artifact readers where the platform allows.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate on broker-cluster rollover or credential expiry.

source_refs.rationale: Provisioning a queue or broker creates client-auth material for workers and inbox/outbox adapters.

usage_constraints: Broker identities must never imply durable truth ownership by themselves.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate on cache-cluster lifecycle or tenant-partition resegmentation.

source_refs.rationale: Provisioning cache and stream-resume state creates service identities.

usage_constraints: Cache credentials do not grant access to raw authority tokens or immutable audit evidence.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate with backend lifecycle changes or collector trust-boundary rollover.

source_refs.rationale: Provisioning telemetry backends creates ingest credentials or collector trust identities.

usage_constraints: Telemetry ingest identities must respect the contract's access separation between operational, security, and privacy signals.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate registry push credentials and signing-key references independently; preserve provenance history across rotations.

source_refs.rationale: Registry, build-signing, and attestation services create delivery credentials and key references.
Signed artifacts and attestation are release-gate requirements.

usage_constraints: Signing keys must remain rooted in the KMS/HSM boundary, not copied into CI runner disks.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate on zone transfer, certificate-automation rollover, or ingress-provider change.

source_refs.rationale: DNS, TLS, WAF, and edge delivery provisioning creates domain-control and certificate-automation identities.

usage_constraints: Do not reuse DNS or certificate automation credentials as generic deployment credentials.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate on runner-image replacement, preview-platform rollover, or release-pipeline hardening changes.

source_refs.rationale: Runner registration and preview-account provisioning create deployment credentials.

usage_constraints: Ephemeral preview credentials must not implicitly grant access to live authority or production secret scopes.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate on certificate expiry, Apple-account rollover, or notarization-boundary change.

source_refs.rationale: Signed and notarized native delivery requires protected Apple-side signing material.
source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 11. Security and runtime posture for the desktop client

usage_constraints: Desktop-signing material must remain outside general CI runner storage and outside product runtime data paths.

credential_records.rotation_or_renewal_rule: Rotate on channel rollover or emergency revocation exercises.

source_refs.rationale: The update channel is a separate publishing boundary with its own release trust.

usage_constraints: Publishing identities must only release already signed and notarized artifacts.
